title: Allow full uppercase text in item name and column headers
excerpt:

posts.author: wallace (Wallace Rafael de Souza)
posts.cookedHtml:
Description

Currently, monday.com automatically formats item names and column headers using sentence‑style capitalisation. As a result, certain terms that must remain fully uppercase, such as acronyms or industry‑standard terminology (e.g., COSHH),are automatically changed to mixed case (e.g., Coshh).
There is currently no option to override this behaviour or enforce custom capitalisation rules.

What are you trying to achieve

I need the ability to display acronyms and technical terms in their correct uppercase form in item column headers. This is important for accuracy, compliance, and industry‑specific terminology.
Being able to type and preserve COSHH, PCR, SOP, and other uppercase terms exactly as written would ensure clarity for all users and prevent misunderstandings in safety‑critical or scientific workflows.
